findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 26 of 8258 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
findings.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
findings.what: The feed contains no fare information.
findings.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

findings.what: This feed describes station pathways, including step-free (elevator) routes.
findings.why: Riders can be routed through the station, and wheelchair users can see whether a step-free route exists.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (87% marked accessible, 13% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is not published.

findings.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
findings.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_same_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance same coordinates (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'same_name_and_description_for_route' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Same name and description for route (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
findings.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
findings.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unsorted_stop_times' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unsorted stop times (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'platform_without_parent_station' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Platform without parent station (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'big_gap_in_service' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Big gap in service (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
findings.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
findings.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
findings.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unknown_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unknown file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 9 kinds of issue across 25329 instances (0 error, 100 warning, 25229 informational).

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data covers the next 48 days.

findings.fix: Check the trip updates endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
findings.what: The trip updates realtime feed failed during sampling.
findings.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

findings.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
findings.what: 18 of 280 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
findings.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

findings.effort: A vendor support ticket with the trip ids attached.
findings.fix: Ask your AVL vendor to check vehicle-to-trip assignments for the flagged trips.
findings.what: 306 of 1230 sampled vehicle positions were far from their assigned route (worst: 11521301 m).
findings.why: A bus shown off its route usually means a wrong trip assignment; riders watch their bus drive the wrong streets.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 9 times: 2 of 3 feeds healthy; 93.6% of scheduled trips had live predictions; 75.1% of vehicles on their route; predictions ran a median of 104s behind schedule.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

top_fixes.fix: Check the trip updates endpoint with your AVL vendor; it should return a fresh GTFS-Realtime protobuf on every request.
top_fixes.what: The trip updates realtime feed failed during sampling.
top_fixes.why: When this feed is down, riders see scheduled times presented as if they were live.

top_fixes.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
top_fixes.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
top_fixes.what: The feed contains no fare information.
top_fixes.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

top_fixes.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
top_fixes.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
top_fixes.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.
